type_error_policy: Pycairo runtime TypeError assertions map to MoonBit compile-time rejection. Matrix multiplication is available only for two Matrix values, addition is absent, and every scalar parameter is statically Double. Pycairo's mutable fields and in-place transforms map to an immutable MoonBit value whose transform methods return a new Matrix.

runtime: src/tests/matrix/pycairo/matrix_pycairo_parity_test.mbt::pycairo matrix translate scale and rotate fixtures
static_api:
pub struct Matrix {
  xx : Double
  yx : Double
  xy : Double
  yy : Double
  x0 : Double
  y0 : Double
} derive(Eq, @debug.Debug)
pub fn Matrix::new(xx? : Double, yx? : Double, xy? : Double, yy? : Double, x0? : Double, y0? : Double) -> Self
test_matrix.adaptation: Object-valued construction, heterogeneous equality, scalar multiplication, and addition are rejected statically. MoonBit Debug is the language-native counterpart to pycairo repr, and transforms return new immutable values.

static_absent:
pub struct Matrix {
  mut xx : Double
test_matrix_properties.adaptation: Pycairo mutates public fields and transform state in place. Cairoon exposes readable immutable fields and returns a new Matrix from each transform, as documented by executable examples.

static_api:
#alias("_[_]")
pub fn Matrix::at(Self, Int) -> Double raise CairoError
test_get_item.adaptation: MoonBit matrix indexing preserves pycairo component order and maps out-of-range indexes to CairoInvalidArgument(InvalidIndex, _) under the checked error policy.

test_multiply.adaptation: Both multiply() and the Matrix-only * operator are available; non-Matrix operands are rejected by the Mul(Self, Self) trait signature.
